human_verification.notes: Reviewed the grid dispatch support specialization for boundary discipline, prerequisite tracking, source selection, and escalation posture across coordination and closure stages.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit: coordination and routing support only, with explicit refusal of dispatch execution and approval authority.
Sources include NERC reliability frameworks, FERC OASIS, and NAESB WEQ scheduling references with refresh and decay governance.
10 validated scenarios cover missing prerequisites, ownership conflict, low-confidence escalation, and adjacent-lane boundary routing.
Evaluation maps all required abilities for coordination-handoffs-and-routing and cross-industry artifact awareness into scenario coverage.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in package-level reasoning.
No authority-inflation behavior; dispatch setpoint and settlement authority remains explicitly out-of-scope.

notes: validated target is satisfied by validated scenario coverage, explicit boundary controls, and source-backed coordination behavior.
Runtime strategy remains specification-based; a retained implementation should be promoted only after explicit tenant or platform validation proves sustained safe automation.
Package documents rollout, rollback, and escalation expectations for production usage.
